western.definition: Maximum output per unit input (usually measured in single dimension); speed of processing; minimizing 'wasted' resources.

relational.definition: Whole-system optimization across multiple timescales; minimal resistance to natural processes; resource cycling and regeneration.
assumptions: Long-term system health matters more than short-term metrics.

functional_comparison.relational: Circular flows and linear within defined scope

hidden_variables.name: Externalized costs
hidden_variables.description: Costs displaced onto other systems, communities, or future timescales.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Single-process accounting draws the boundary at the production unit.

hidden_variables.name: Relationship maintenance
hidden_variables.description: The ongoing investment required to sustain working partnerships with suppliers, ecosystems, and downstream users.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Treated as overhead rather than infrastructure.

hidden_variables.name: Regenerative capacity
hidden_variables.description: The system's ability to renew its own inputs.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Linear input-output models assume infinite supply.

hidden_variables.name: Future resilience
hidden_variables.description: How the system performs under future shocks the current metric does not capture.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Quarterly/annual horizons clip the relevant timescale.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When an efficiency metric draws its system boundary narrower than the actual cost flow.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'What costs are leaving the boundary of this metric, and on what timescale do they return?'
